item.title: Work begins on Whiston Brook Flood Alleviation Scheme
item.description: Work has now started on the Whiston Brook Flood Alleviation Scheme, to help reduce the risk of flooding in Whiston and better protect homes, businesses and local roads.

item.title: Support for men’s mental health rolls out on Rotherham’s streets
item.description: Rotherham Council is showing its support for charity Andy’s Man Club by displaying a new mental health message on the side of one of its waste collection vehicles.

item.title: Templeborough business zone work underway
item.description: Work to develop a new business zone alongside a food and drink offer at an iconic tourist attraction in Rotherham is underway.

item.title: Construction work starts on 31 new Council homes in Eastwood
item.description: Work has started on building 31 new Rotherham Council homes for local people to rent in Eastwood.

item.title: Rotherham schools set to benefit from capital investment programme
item.description: Rotherham Council’s Cabinet is set to consider proposals for over £4.1million of grant funding for a range of capital works for schools across the borough when it meets on Monday 6 July 2026.

item.title: “I still wake up every day and can’t believe I live here” – New Council homes making a difference to the lives of Rotherham residents
item.description: “I still wake up every day and can’t believe I live here” - Tenants have spoken of the difference their homes are making as the Council continues to provide 1,000 new Council homes for local people.

item.title: Ambitious town centre regeneration plan set to move forward
item.description: A major regeneration programme that could transform five key sites in Rotherham town centre is set to move into its next phase.

item.title: Rotherham wins national award for Children’s Capital of Culture
item.description: Rotherham has won a national award for its Children’s Capital of Culture programme.

item.title: Rotherham’s annual Armed Forces Day event remembers veterans
item.description: Rotherham will once again unite to show its appreciation for the Armed Forces community at the annual Armed Forces Day celebration on Saturday 20 June.

item.title: Rotherham set for ‘decade of opportunity’ as bold new plan launches
item.description: Residents across Rotherham will benefit from better jobs and skills development, faster connections, stronger communities and thriving high streets under a new 10-year plan unveiled this week.

item.title: Rotherham Council opens new state-of-the-art day-care centre for residents with learning disabilities
item.description: A new £6.4 million day centre to support people with a range of needs, including learning disabilities and autism, has been opened in Rotherham.

item.title: Smoke and Pepper set to spice up Forge Island this summer
item.description: A new food outlet promising bold flavours and a fresh dining experience is set to open its doors at Forge Island later this summer.

item.title: New support for Carers launched in Rotherham during Carers Week
item.description: Rotherham Council is marking Carers Week with the launch of Carer Connect, a new website and digital assistant designed to help carers access trusted advice, local support and practical information.

item.title: Date announced for Market Hall opening as indoor market prepares to move this summer
item.description: Rotherham’s new Market Hall will open on Friday 10 July, as the indoor market temporarily relocates during planned improvement works.

item.title: Statement from Rotherham Council following yesterday's incident at Rother Valley Country Park
item.description: Andrew Bramidge, Executive Director of Regeneration and Environment at Rotherham Council said: "We are deeply saddened by the tragic events at Rother Valley Country Park over the Bank Holiday weekend.

item.title: Rotherham residents embrace new discount swim scheme
item.description: Over 500 swimmers in Rotherham have benefited from a discount scheme which launched last month thanks to investment from Rotherham Council.

item.title: Remember the importance of water safety when near open water sites
item.description: Rotherham Council is urging residents to be extra careful when in and around open water during warm weather.

item.title: Councillor Haroon Rashid has been appointed as Mayor of Rotherham for 2026–2027
item.description: Councillor Haroon Rashid has been appointed as Mayor of Rotherham for 2026–2027, becoming the borough’s First Citizen.

item.title: This is Fostering – Celebrating Rotherham’s Foster Carers this Foster Care Fortnight
item.description: Rotherham Council is celebrating the amazing support and care that the borough’s Foster Carers give to local children during Foster Care Fortnight.

item.title: Rotherham Council prosecutes landlord over unsafe living conditions
item.description: Rotherham Council has successfully prosecuted a landlord after serious hazards were identified at a privately rented property in the borough.
